title: HTML Links
content: ### HTML Links Links are defined with the `` tag (anchor). --- ### Basic Link ```html Visit Google ``` The `href` attribute specifies the destination URL. --- ### Open in New Tab Use `target="_blank"`: ```html Open Google in new tab ``` --- ### Target Values | Value | Description | |-------|-------------| | `_self` | Same tab (default) | | `_blank` | New tab/window | | `_parent` | Parent frame | | `_top` | Full body of window | --- ### Link States Links have these default states (can be styled with CSS): - `a:link` -- normal, unvisited link (blue, underlined) - `a:visited` -- visited link (purple, underlined) - `a:hover` -- mouse over link - `a:active` -- being clicked (red) --- ### Image as Link ```html ``` --- ### Email and Phone Links ```html Send Email Call Us ``` --- ### Title Attribute (Tooltip) ```html Home ``` > **Best Practice:** Always use meaningful link text. Avoid "Click here" -- use descriptive text that tells users where the link goes.
codeExample:
Visit Google
Open Wikipedia (new tab)
Send Email
Call: 123-456-7890

quiz.question: Which attribute specifies the link destination?
